snippets.title: MLflow: full run with params, metrics, and artifact
snippets.code:
import mlflow
import json
from pathlib import Path

mlflow.set_experiment("churn-baseline")

with mlflow.start_run(run_name="xgb-v1") as run:
    run_id = run.info.run_id
    mlflow.log_param("learning_rate", 0.05)
    mlflow.log_param("seed", 42)
    mlflow.log_param("data_version", "snap-2024-06-01")

    for epoch in range(3):
        train_loss = 0.5 - 0.1 * epoch
        val_auc = 0.80 + 0.03 * epoch
        mlflow.log_metric("train_loss", train_loss, step=epoch)
        mlflow.log_metric("val_auc", val_auc, step=epoch)

    report = {"run_id": run_id, "best_val_auc": 0.86}
    Path("report.json").write_text(json.dumps(report))
    mlflow.log_artifact("report.json")
    print("logged run:", run_id)
snippets.explanation: One run context, params logged once, metrics repeated with `step`, artifact uploaded. `run_id` is the primary key for later comparison.

snippets.title: MLflow: compare runs programmatically
snippets.code:
from mlflow.tracking import MlflowClient

client = MlflowClient()
experiment = client.get_experiment_by_name("churn-baseline")
runs = client.search_runs(
    experiment_ids=[experiment.experiment_id],
    filter_string="tags.stage = 'candidate'",
    order_by=["metrics.val_auc DESC"],
    max_results=5,
)

for r in runs:
    print(r.info.run_id, r.data.params.get("learning_rate"), r.data.metrics.get("val_auc"))
snippets.explanation: Search and sort runs by metric; pull params and metrics for a comparison table without opening the UI.

snippets.title: W&B: init, config, metrics, and artifact
snippets.code:
import wandb

run = wandb.init(
    project="churn-model",
    name="xgb-v1",
    tags=["baseline", "candidate"],
    config={"learning_rate": 0.05, "seed": 42, "data_version": "snap-2024-06-01"},
)

try:
    for epoch in range(3):
        wandb.log({
            "train_loss": 0.5 - 0.1 * epoch,
            "val_auc": 0.80 + 0.03 * epoch,
            "epoch": epoch,
        })
    wandb.save("report.json")
finally:
    run.finish()
snippets.explanation: Config holds params; `wandb.log` streams metrics. Tags support UI filters. Always `finish()` so the run closes cleanly.

snippets.title: Log the same val metric on train and val splits
snippets.code:
import mlflow

with mlflow.start_run():
    # Params: which split policy was used (see Train/Val/Test Splits)
    mlflow.log_param("split_seed", 42)
    mlflow.log_param("val_fraction", 0.2)

    # Metrics: prefix or separate keys — stay consistent across all runs
    mlflow.log_metric("train_f1", 0.91)
    mlflow.log_metric("val_f1", 0.84)  # compare runs on val_f1, not train_f1
snippets.explanation: Consistent metric keys (`val_f1`) and logged split params make run comparison meaningful. Metric semantics are in Tracking Metrics.
